narrative: Dear Alex, imagine our admissions model as a wise mentor who's reviewed thousands of stories like yours, piecing together patterns from past students to spot who thrives on the bar exam journey. It looked at your path and saw a 54% chance of stumbling at the finish line, which is why we had to pause on your application—it's a tough call, but one aimed at protecting your time and dreams. The biggest shadow in your story was your Year 3 decile of 2.0, placing you near the bottom of your class when it matters most for building exam-ready skills. Right behind that, your Year 1 decile of 3.0 set an early tone of middling performance that echoed through your law school trajectory. Your LSAT score of 35.5 added to the concern, signaling a foundation that hasn't always clicked with the intense reasoning the bar demands. Even with your solid undergraduate GPA of 3.5 and upper-middle family income, those pulled in the same direction, as students in similar spots have often faced steeper climbs without extra momentum. Your background as a white male pursuing full-time studies fit patterns where others with these traits struggled more than expected to push past hurdles. It's not that any one piece defines you—it's how they wove together into a picture of risk that the model couldn't ignore. This isn't the end of your story; many turn things around by targeting those early deciles with focused prep or retakes. You've got strengths like that UGPA that could shine brighter with a boost. We see your potential, Alex, and hope this nudge helps you rewrite the next chapter stronger. Keep pushing—you're closer than it feels.
